# Abstract
We review multimodal learning approaches for audio visual understanding. The survey synthesizes datasets, models, and evaluation practices.

# Introduction
Multimodal systems combine complementary signals to improve perception. Research has accelerated with large scale datasets.

# Methods
We curated 120 papers and grouped them by fusion strategy. Evaluation metrics were standardized for comparison.

# Results
Late fusion dominates recent benchmarks, while early fusion offers robustness. Self supervised objectives are increasingly common.

Figure 1. Taxonomy of multimodal fusion strategies.

# Discussion
Benchmarks emphasize accuracy but under report latency and energy costs. New metrics are needed for real time systems.

Table 1. Representative datasets and tasks.

# Conclusion
The field benefits from consistent reporting and open benchmarks. Future work should address efficiency and bias.

# References
[1] I. Reviewer. Multimodal learning survey. 2021.
[2] J. Reviewer. Audio visual benchmarks. 2020.
